title: CoAP option iterator uses saturating_add for current_number, masking protocol violations
description:
# Problem
In `rust/lichen-coap/src/codec.rs:359`:

```rust
self.current_number = self.current_number.saturating_add(delta);
```

If a malformed CoAP message has option deltas that overflow u16, `saturating_add` silently clamps to 65535. This means:
1. Multiple different malformed inputs produce identical parsed output
2. A round-trip (parse → serialize → parse) will produce different results
3. Security filters based on option numbers can be bypassed

## What goes wrong
A malicious CoAP message with crafted option deltas could:
- Make option number 11 (Uri-Path) appear as option 65535 (or vice versa)
- Bypass URI-based authorization by having options parse to different numbers than intended

## Trigger
CoAP message with options: delta=30000, delta=40000. 
After first: current_number=30000
After second: current_number=65535 (clamped) instead of 70000 (error)

## Fix
Return `Err(CoapError::OptionNumberOverflow)` when `delta + current_number > u16::MAX`.
close_reason: Fixed: Changed saturating_add to checked_add with explicit error return (OptionNumberOverflow) to prevent silent protocol violations. Added test case for overflow detection.
